title: Status Lines, Informational Responses, Content Rules, and Response Parsing · Runtime Contract: Response Retry Ambiguity
summary: A client parser accepts malformed status codes, stops at 100 Continue, assigns content to HEAD and 204, treats successful CONNECT as ordinary HTTP, and retries after an incomplete response. Planned artifact: Add and verify the response retry ambiguity increment in response control-data and content decision parser. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify incomplete, malformed, unsolicited, and connection-lost responses without blindly replaying methods whose effects may have occurred.
Transition a successful CONNECT response from HTTP message parsing to opaque tunnel bytes without losing buffered data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Response Retry Ambiguity · Theory Response Retry Ambiguity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 6710 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound informational responses and transition HEAD, no-content, and CONNECT responses correctly; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Response Retry Ambiguity contract for the supplied data.
